features: TL-UL CSR access compliance for 32-bit register reads/writes
AES-128 CBC encrypt/decrypt functional flow with IV handling
128-bit key programming across four consecutive CSR writes
128-bit data input/output programming across four consecutive CSR accesses
Status polling for output_valid and input_ready behavior
Key/IV/data clear behavior via trigger bits
Reset behavior and CSR default state
Register field packing and little-endian word ordering
TL-UL ready/response handshake stability under backpressure
Alert output observation for fatal error conditions
FSM state progression across IDLE, KEY_EXPAND, INIT_CBC, ROUND, and DONE

directed_tests.name: reset_and_csr_sanity
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset deassertion, TL-UL accessibility, readable/writable CSR defaults, and that status/control registers respond without protocol errors.

directed_tests.name: aes_ecb_encrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program a known AES-128 key and plaintext, trigger ECB encryption, and compare ciphertext against a golden model.

directed_tests.name: aes_ecb_decrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program a known AES-128 key and ciphertext, trigger ECB decryption, and compare recovered plaintext against a golden model.

directed_tests.name: aes_cbc_encrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program key, IV, and plaintext, trigger CBC encryption, and verify ciphertext plus IV update behavior against a golden model.

directed_tests.name: aes_cbc_decrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program key, IV, and ciphertext, trigger CBC decryption, and verify plaintext recovery and IV update behavior.

directed_tests.name: back_to_back_block_operations
directed_tests.intent: Issue consecutive start triggers with new input data and confirm the DUT accepts the next block when input_ready indicates availability.

directed_tests.name: clear_triggers
directed_tests.intent: Exercise key_iv_data_in_clear and data_out_clear independently and confirm registers are zeroized without corrupting unrelated control/status behavior.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_completion_flow
directed_tests.intent: Enable/observe completion interrupt behavior and confirm intr_o asserts on done and deasserts according to the design's completion semantics.

directed_tests.name: tlul_backpressure_and_wait_states
directed_tests.intent: Apply host-side ready deassertion and randomized TL-UL request spacing to verify stable request/response handling under backpressure.

random_tests.name: csr_fuzz_smoke
random_tests.intent: Randomize legal CSR writes and reads across key, IV, data, control, trigger, and status registers while checking TL-UL protocol correctness and basic register coherency.

random_tests.name: mode_operation_randomized
random_tests.intent: Randomly select ECB/CBC and encrypt/decrypt operations with constrained legal sequences, then compare outputs to a Python AES reference model.

random_tests.name: multi_block_stream_randomized
random_tests.intent: Generate short random block streams to stress back-to-back operation handling, output retention, and IV chaining in CBC mode.

random_tests.name: reset_interruption_randomized
random_tests.intent: Assert reset at random points during idle and active operation to verify safe recovery, no protocol deadlock, and clean post-reset CSR state.

risk_areas.reason: Core functionality depends on correct key expansion, round transformations, and inverse cipher behavior; a single mismatch breaks all cryptographic outputs.

risk_areas.area: CBC IV chaining and update semantics
risk_areas.reason: CBC mode adds stateful dependency across blocks and is prone to off-by-one or ordering bugs in IV capture/update.
FSM transitions and input_ready/output_valid timing are likely to expose race conditions or stale data reuse.
Incorrect register addressing, byte ordering, or handshake behavior can block all software interaction and is fast to validate early.
Simultaneous clear/start behavior is explicitly underdefined and may reveal implementation-specific hazards or unintended side effects.
Completion interrupt and fatal alert outputs are externally visible integration points that may be miswired or incorrectly timed.
Asynchronous reset during active processing can expose state retention bugs, though it is typically less complex than algorithmic correctness.
The design claims back-to-back support, but exact latency is implementation-dependent; verify only observable readiness and completion behavior within the time budget.
